claim: Metallic hydrogen was finally demonstrated in experimental conditions to be a room temperature superconductor.
Unlike iron oxide nanoparticles, iron sulfide nanoparticles do not possess intrinsic peroxidase activity.
Absence of superconductivity in multilayer FeSe films is due to insufficient carrier transfer from STO substrate.
A next generation rechargeable lithium ion high-voltage cell was developed that uses high-temperature molten salt for its electrolytes.
Higher Li capacity in carbon nanotubes was obtained by making all the interstitial sites (inter-shell van der Waals spaces, intertube channels, and inner cores) accessible for Li intercalation.
Capacity enhancement is related more to carbon defects than N-doping in N-doped carbon nanofibers.
Toluene forms a thin, stable, porous, and conducting interface that reduces the solubility of polysulfides, preventing them from dissolving into the bulk of the electrolyte.
New lithium ion batteries with Li3/GDY anodes demonstrate improved storage capacity and rate capability in high performance applications such as vehicles and power backups.
Potassium vanadium fluorophosphate (KVPO 4 F) has been discovered as a new anode material for potassium-ion batteries at low operating voltages.
New sodium ion batteries directly reuse lithium ion battery technology including LCO, LMO and LFP cathodes and natural graphite anodes.
A new high-voltage battery uses EC-based electrolypes for its cathode, leveraging decades of commercialized research.
